summary.confidence_statement: Calibration and whitebox-governance primitives are present, but world-class prediction accuracy is not commercially claimable until resolved-outcome ledgers, reliability curves, hosted dry-runs, security boundaries, and benchmark comparisons are attached.

requirements.label: TypeScript calibration fitting uses isotonic or prior-smoothed calibration.
requirements.rationale: Local and Edge code need a reusable calibration model before accuracy claims can be measured.

requirements.label: Calibration metrics include Brier loss before and after calibration.
requirements.rationale: Brier loss is the minimum scoring primitive for binary forecast credibility.

requirements.label: ML service can refresh persisted calibration models from resolved outcomes.
requirements.rationale: A production learning loop needs an operational calibration refresh endpoint.

requirements.label: ML service stores sample size and raw/calibrated Brier metrics.
requirements.rationale: Calibration claims need sample-size and score metadata, not only adjusted probabilities.

requirements.label: Whitebox release helper computes Brier scores per resolved forecast variant.
requirements.rationale: Consensus-policy promotion should be score-backed by resolved outcomes.

requirements.label: Promotion is blocked until a minimum resolved sample size is available.
requirements.rationale: Small-sample challenger wins must not drive automatic promotion.

requirements.label: Scheduled whitebox job backfills resolved forecast evaluations.
requirements.rationale: The app needs a repeatable backfill path from resolved forecasts to evaluation rows.

requirements.label: Release evaluation endpoint summarizes challenger performance.
requirements.rationale: Governed releases need a dry-run/evaluation surface before promotion.

requirements.label: Release promotion endpoint requires threshold evidence unless forced.
requirements.rationale: Manual or automatic policy changes need explicit gates.

requirements.label: Calibration refresh Edge function proxies authorized calls to the ML service.
requirements.rationale: Hosted operations need a service boundary rather than direct client writes.

requirements.label: Drift evaluation Edge function proxies authorized calls to the ML service.
requirements.rationale: Forecast reliability needs drift monitoring when evidence distributions shift.

requirements.label: Consensus aggregation incorporates user Brier history and reliability.
requirements.rationale: A sellable forecast workflow needs a performance-weighted consensus lane.

requirements.label: Schema stores whitebox release state, evaluations, and decisions.
requirements.rationale: Resolved-outcome evaluation needs durable tables, not transient logs.

requirements.label: Schema stores calibration models, drift signals, and shadow predictions.
requirements.rationale: Calibration and drift artifacts need first-class database tables.

requirements.label: Unit tests cover prior-smoothed calibration and drift flags.
requirements.rationale: Scientific primitives need deterministic regression coverage.

requirements.label: Whitebox tests cover challenger promotion on resolved samples.
requirements.rationale: Release-governance logic needs direct tests for promotion decisions.

requirements.label: Resolved-outcome calibration ledger harness computes Brier metrics and reliability bins from approved exports.
requirements.rationale: Commercial accuracy claims need a repeatable report path from resolved forecast exports to calibration evidence.

evidence_gates.evidence: docs/launch-readiness/resolved-forecast-calibration-ledger-2026-06-06.json verifies the ledger mechanism as a sample fixture, but is not launch proof.
evidence_gates.fix: Generate a resolved forecast ledger with included/excluded question rules, sample size, Brier score, calibration status, and benchmark baseline.

evidence_gates.evidence: docs/launch-readiness/resolved-forecast-calibration-ledger-2026-06-06.json includes reliability bins for a sample fixture only.
evidence_gates.fix: Add probability-bin reliability curves or equivalent calibration buckets for resolved binary forecasts.

evidence_gates.evidence: No hosted calibration-refresh, whitebox-scheduled, release-evaluation, or release-promotion smoke output is captured in this launch evidence set.
evidence_gates.fix: After owner-approved deploy and secrets, run hosted dry-run jobs and attach logs/screenshots without exposing credentials.

evidence_gates.evidence: docs/launch-readiness/forecast-benchmark-comparison-2026-06-06.json verifies benchmark comparison mechanics with sample-only data.
evidence_gates.fix: Compare app forecasts against a trivial base rate, uncalibrated model, human/community baseline where available, and a published benchmark protocol.

evidence_gates.evidence: RLS static audit still reports relevant P1 findings for whitebox/shadow/forecast-adjacent tables.
evidence_gates.fix: Classify calibration/evaluation tables as public, authenticated, owner-bound, or service-only, then verify policies against hosted Supabase state.

best_practice_alignment.repo_evidence: Brier scoring, resolved outcome backfill, and challenger release evaluation exist.
best_practice_alignment.missing: External benchmark protocol, model/human baseline comparison, and public resolved sample report.

best_practice_alignment.framework: NIST AI RMF Measure and Manage
best_practice_alignment.repo_evidence: Calibration metrics, drift signals, and release decisions create monitoring primitives.
best_practice_alignment.missing: Operational measurement report, incident thresholds, owner sign-off, and hosted monitoring proof.

best_practice_alignment.repo_evidence: Governed promotion/hold decisions and shadow/challenger tables exist.
best_practice_alignment.missing: Named AI management process, periodic review cadence, and externally reviewable records.

best_practice_alignment.repo_evidence: Local dependency audit and unsafe eval hardening are complete in the launch evidence set.
best_practice_alignment.missing: Tenant/object-boundary proof for evaluation data and hosted auth/RLS smoke checks.

top_blockers: No approved resolved-outcome export ledger with enough samples for commercial accuracy claims.
No approved reliability curve or probability-bin calibration report.
No hosted dry-run proof for calibration refresh, whitebox scheduled backfill, release evaluation, or promotion hold.
No approved benchmark comparison against real human/community/pro/external baselines.
RLS and tenant-boundary proof for calibration/evaluation/shadow data remains unresolved.
recommended_next_actions: Add a local resolved-forecast calibration report generator that reads only sanitized/sample data or hosted exports approved by the owner.
Use reliability bins and Brier decomposition before making any prediction-superiority claim.
Keep market copy at "calibration-aware decision support" until the ledger shows enough resolved outcomes.
Run hosted dry-runs only after secrets/deploy are owner-approved, and redact all credentials from evidence artifacts.
Fold calibration-adjacent RLS tables into the owner-approved policy-hardening queue.
